TSLA: Tesla and the Trillion-Dollar Question. This article examines the likelihood of "dual-top-decile" stocks, like Tesla, achieving multifold growth given their already large market capitalizations and high multiples. Through fifty years of data analysis, it reveals that such stocks rarely quintuple over a decade and often underperform, with rare winners characterized by durable profitability and capital discipline rather than just fast growth. The research provides historical context for evaluating today's megacap valuations and suggests a focus on resilient compounders alongside smaller, "lottery-like" stocks.

Tesla pays Nevada $200K in fines for operating battery recycling equipment without permit. Tesla has been fined $200,000 by the Nevada Division of Environmental Protection (NDEP) for operating battery recycling equipment at its Gigafactory without a permit. The equipment, used to recover materials like lithium and copper, had been operating since at least May 2021 before the violation was discovered in February 2023. This settlement is part of a series of environmental fines and violations involving Elon Musk's companies in Nevada and California.

Tesla suffers major setback as key employee joins mass exodus: 'Leaving the sinking ship'. Tesla is reportedly experiencing a mass exodus of high-ranking employees, including the Director of Vehicle Operations and Engineering at Fremont, Benjamin Bate. This trend is attributed to growing discontent with the company's shift towards robotics and AI, and a perception that Tesla is no longer innovating in the automotive sector. The loss of talent and declining sales could negatively impact Tesla customers and the company's competitive standing against rising EV manufacturers like China's BYD.

Suit claims Tesla Cybertruck parts severely injured a Gigafactory Texas worker. A former safety supervisor at Tesla's Gigafactory Texas is suing the company, alleging he sustained a traumatic brain injury and other severe physical harm when 150 pounds of unsecured Cybertruck parts fell on him. The lawsuit claims unsafe workplace practices, including obstructed pedestrian walkways and a policy prioritizing vehicle traffic over worker safety, led to the incident. The plaintiff is seeking over $1 million in damages, and the suit highlights previous safety violations against Tesla and other Elon Musk-owned companies.

Morgan Stanley CEO received a 32% raise in 2025. Morgan Stanley CEO Ted Pick received a substantial 32% raise in 2025, bringing his compensation package to $45 million, following the company's strongest year ever in terms of earnings per share. Other tech and finance CEOs, including Satya Nadella, Jensen Huang, and Jamie Dimon, also saw significant pay increases, though Elon Musk's potential Tesla compensation could dwarf them all. Pick's bonus is largely deferred in equity, linking his future earnings to the stock's performance.
TGT: American Small Business Sues to Break Trump’s Tariff Grip - Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ:COST), Target (NYSE:TGT). Detroit Axle CEO Mike Musheinesh has sued the Trump administration over its import tariff policies, which dramatically increased his company's tariff bill. Musheinesh's lawsuit challenges the authority to tax via executive order and the removal of the de minimis exemption, tying into the Supreme Court case V.O.S. Selections. Major corporations like Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ: COST) and Target (NYSE: TGT) are also challenging the tariffs, with a critical Supreme Court ruling expected soon.

Target makes $7.3M land acquisition in Charlotte region. Target Corp. has acquired 15 acres of land near Rock Hill in the Charlotte region for $7.3 million. This acquisition suggests that Target will likely be the anchor tenant for a new retail development in the area, following previous discussions and plans for retail and residential projects in York County.

Target’s new CEO wants a refresh at the store level. Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, is implementing significant changes by investing in store labor and expanding customer experience training for employees. This strategy aims to improve efficiency and customer satisfaction, addressing issues like cluttered stores and inconsistent merchandise availability. The company is also undergoing executive leadership changes to support these new initiatives.

Target is testing contextual ads in ChatGPT. Target is initiating a pilot program with OpenAI to test contextual advertising within ChatGPT, making it one of the first retailers to do so. Starting this month, sponsored ads from Target and its media business, Roundel, will appear alongside shopping-related conversations in the AI chatbot. The ads will be clearly labeled, triggered by keywords in user prompts, and will not influence ChatGPT's responses, aiming to gauge advertising effectiveness in conversational AI environments while maintaining user trust.

Target’s new CEO wants a refresh at the store level. Target's new CEO, Michael Fiddelke, is initiating a store-level refresh by investing in labor and customer experience training for employees, alongside a restructuring that eliminates 500 roles at the supply chain and district levels. This strategy aims to improve efficiency and address declining store standards and customer complaints regarding clutter and inconsistent merchandise availability. The changes also include appointing new chief merchandising and operating officers.
SBUX: Starbucks Corporation $SBUX Shares Sold by Cibc World Market Inc.. Cibc World Market Inc. significantly reduced its stake in Starbucks by 56.1% in Q3 2025, selling 274,077 shares. Despite the reduction, Starbucks stock maintains a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts, with 72.29% ownership by institutional investors. The company recently missed EPS expectations but exceeded revenue forecasts and provided FY2026 EPS guidance.
DIS: InterDigital Secures Fifth Injunction Against Disney Over Patent Infringement in Germany. InterDigital has obtained a fifth injunction against Disney from the Munich Regional Court in Germany for infringing a patent related to HEVC compression technology. This ruling allows InterDigital to enforce the injunction, though Disney can appeal. InterDigital, a pioneer in wireless, video, and AI technology, believes Disney should sign an agreement recognizing their innovation that has supported Disney's streaming business.

InterDigital awarded fifth injunction against Disney. InterDigital has been awarded its fifth injunction against Disney by a German court, this time concerning infringement of an HEVC compression technology patent. This latest ruling follows previous injunctions in Germany and Brazil related to various video technologies. InterDigital urges Disney to sign a licensing agreement that acknowledges the value of its innovations, particularly given InterDigital's contributions to Disney's streaming business.

InterDigital awarded fifth injunction against Disney. InterDigital has been awarded a fifth injunction against Disney by a German court for infringement of a patent related to HEVC compression technology. This marks another victory for InterDigital in its ongoing intellectual property disputes with Disney, with previous injunctions issued in Germany and Brazil for various video technologies. InterDigital's Chief Legal Officer urged Disney to agree to a licensing agreement that reflects the value of their innovations used in Disney's streaming business.

United States shares lower at close of trade; Dow Jones Industrial Average down 1.34% By Investing.com. US equities closed lower on Thursday, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average dropping 1.34%, the S&P 500 declining 1.57%, and the NASDAQ Composite falling 2.04%. Losses were led by the Technology, Basic Materials, and Oil & Gas sectors. Notable movers included Cisco Systems Inc, Walt Disney Company, and Apple Inc among the biggest losers, while Walmart Inc and McDonald’s Corporation were among the gainers.

Disney to pay $2.75 million to settle alleged violations of the California Consumer Privacy Act. Walt Disney Co. has agreed to pay $2.75 million to settle allegations that it violated the California Consumer Privacy Act by not fully complying with users' requests to opt out of data sharing on its streaming services. The settlement, which is the largest under the state privacy act, will require Disney to implement a more consumer-friendly process for data opt-out requests. California Attorney General Rob Bonta emphasized the importance of businesses making privacy rights easy for consumers to exercise.

DIS Stock Price, Forecast & Analysis | WALT DISNEY CO/THE (NYSE:DIS).
